-{"id":"beads_rust-7eiw","title":"[P2] Fuzz: content_hash harness generates issues without validation gate","description":"Gap: fuzz/fuzz_targets/content_hash.rs constructs Issue structs from raw fuzz bytes via issue_from_bytes() (using ByteCursor helpers from common.rs) and computes content hashes on them without any validation. The harness verifies hash determinism, JSON formatting stability, unknown field tolerance, empty optional equivalence, NUL-space equivalence, and ignored fields — all important hash invariants — but does not verify that the generated issues are valid according to IssueValidator::validate(). This means the harness may be computing hashes on issues with invalid IDs, oversized titles, out-of-range priorities, or broken timestamp invariants. If content_hash() behaves differently on invalid vs valid issues (e.g., panics on certain field combinations), the harness won't detect it. Target fuzz harness: content_hash. Invariant to assert: partition fuzz-generated issues into valid (pass IssueValidator::validate()) and invalid; verify hash properties hold for both partitions but especially that valid issues always produce a well-formed 64-char hex hash. Compare with markdown_import.rs:34 which already demonstrates the validate-then-hash pattern.","status":"open","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:51.142898784Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:18:51.142898784Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-joe7","title":"EPIC: Testing Infrastructure & CI Pipeline","description":"# Testing Infrastructure & CI Pipeline

## Background & Rationale

Based on the review of existing beads, a comprehensive testing infrastructure is needed to ensure all features are properly validated. This epic consolidates testing requirements across all beads.

## Goals
Establish a robust, automated testing pipeline that catches regressions, validates multi-platform compatibility, and produces detailed logs for debugging.

## Deliverables

### 1. CI/CD Pipeline (.github/workflows/)
- `ci.yml` - Main CI: lint, test, build on every PR
- `release.yml` - Release automation with checksums
- `install-test.yml` - Multi-platform installer tests
- `docs.yml` - Documentation validation

### 2. Test Matrix
| Platform | Arch | Test Type |
|----------|------|-----------|
| ubuntu-22.04 | x86_64 | unit, integration, e2e |
| ubuntu-22.04-arm64 | aarch64 | unit, integration, e2e |
| macos-13 | x86_64 | unit, integration, e2e |
| macos-14 | arm64 | unit, integration, e2e |
| windows-latest | x86_64 | unit, integration |

### 3. Test Categories
- **Unit Tests** (tests/unit/) - Fast, isolated, mockable
- **Integration Tests** (tests/integration/) - Database, file I/O
- **E2E Tests** (tests/e2e/) - Full CLI workflow
- **Property Tests** (using proptest) - Fuzzing for edge cases

### 4. Test Utilities
- `tests/common/mod.rs` - Shared test helpers
- `tests/fixtures/` - Test data files
- `TempDir` helper for isolated test environments
- Mock server for GitHub API tests

### 5. Coverage & Reporting
- Code coverage with `cargo tarpaulin`
- Coverage badge in README
- HTML coverage report artifact
- Minimum threshold: 80%

### 6. Logging Infrastructure
- Test logs: `tests/logs/<test_name>_<timestamp>.log`
- Structured JSON logs for CI parsing
- Log retention policy (keep last 10 runs)

### 7. Bash Script Testing (bats-core)
- Install bats-core in CI
- Test suite for install.sh
- Mock external commands

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] All tests pass on all platforms
- [ ] Coverage >= 80%
- [ ] CI runs in < 10 minutes
- [ ] Logs are accessible as artifacts
- [ ] Failed tests produce actionable diagnostics

## Technical Notes

### Cargo.toml Test Dependencies
```toml
[dev-dependencies]
assert_cmd = "2.0"
predicates = "3.0"
tempfile = "3.8"
mockito = "1.2"
proptest = "1.4"
```

### CI Cache Strategy
- Cache ~/.cargo/registry
- Cache target/
- Hash Cargo.lock for cache key

## Dependencies
- All feature beads implicitly depend on this for validation","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"epic","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T20:04:57.452657413Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:48.187066949Z","closed_at":"2026-01-17T16:09:48.187066949Z","close_reason":"Duplicate bead (second- prefix) - original is beads_rust-ums","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-k0w","title":"q (quick capture) Command Implementation","description":"# q (quick capture) Command

## Purpose
Fast create path for agents: creates an issue and prints **only the ID** on stdout. Ignores `--json` (matches bd behavior).

## CLI
```
br q <title...>
```
Flags:
- `--priority/-p` (default 2)
- `--type/-t` (default task)
- `--labels/-l` (repeatable)

## Behavior
- Title is the joined args.
- Uses same create validation and ID generation as `create`.
- Adds labels best-effort (warnings only).
- Schedules auto-flush if enabled.

## Output
- Always one line: `<id>`

## Acceptance Criteria
- Output is ID-only regardless of `--json`.
- Mirrors bd quick capture behavior.

## Tests
- `q` outputs only ID and creates issue with correct type/priority.","status":"closed","priority":3,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:17:46.288968908Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T14:13:47.869113116Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T14:13:47.869113116Z","close_reason":"Implemented q command","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-k0y","title":"doctor Command Implementation","description":"## Overview
Implement the `br doctor` command to diagnose project health issues and suggest fixes. This is essential for troubleshooting corrupted databases, orphaned references, and configuration problems.

## CLI Interface
```
br doctor [OPTIONS]

Options:
  --fix                     Attempt to automatically fix issues
  --check <CHECK>           Run specific check only (can repeat)
  --skip <CHECK>            Skip specific check (can repeat)
  --json                    Output as JSON
  --robot                   Machine-readable output

Available checks:
  schema       - Database schema integrity
  refs         - Referential integrity (deps, labels)
  blocked      - Blocked cache consistency
  fts          - FTS5 index consistency
  orphans      - Orphaned dependencies/labels
  config       - Configuration validity
  jsonl        - JSONL file consistency
```

## Technical Requirements

### Health Check Framework
```rust
pub trait HealthCheck: Send + Sync {
    fn name(&self) -> &str;
    fn description(&self) -> &str;
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult>;
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult>;
}

pub enum CheckResult {
    Ok,
    Warning(String),
    Error(String),
}

pub enum FixResult {
    Fixed(String),
    CannotFix(String),
    NoFixNeeded,
}
```

### Individual Checks

#### Schema Check
```rust
impl HealthCheck for SchemaCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        // Verify all expected tables exist
        let expected = ["issues", "dependencies", "labels", "comments", 
                        "events", "config", "metadata", "dirty_issues",
                        "blocked_issues_cache", "child_counters"];
        for table in expected {
            if !storage.table_exists(table)? {
                return Ok(CheckResult::Error(format!("Missing table: {}", table)));
            }
        }
        // Verify pragma values
        let journal = storage.pragma_query_value("journal_mode")?;
        if journal != "wal" {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Warning("journal_mode not WAL".into()));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
}
```

#### Referential Integrity Check
```rust
impl HealthCheck for RefIntegrityCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        // Find dependencies pointing to non-existent issues
        let orphans = storage.query::<String>(
            "SELECT d.issue_id FROM dependencies d 
             LEFT JOIN issues i ON d.depends_on_id = i.id 
             WHERE i.id IS NULL"
        )?;
        if !orphans.is_empty() {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Error(
                format!("{} orphaned dependencies found", orphans.len())
            ));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
    
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult> {
        let deleted = storage.execute(
            "DELETE FROM dependencies WHERE depends_on_id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM issues)"
        )?;
        Ok(FixResult::Fixed(format!("Deleted {} orphaned dependencies", deleted)))
    }
}
```

#### Blocked Cache Consistency
```rust
impl HealthCheck for BlockedCacheCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        // Recompute blocked issues and compare with cache
        let computed = storage.compute_blocked_issues()?;
        let cached = storage.get_blocked_from_cache()?;
        if computed != cached {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Error("Blocked cache out of sync".into()));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
    
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult> {
        storage.rebuild_blocked_issues_cache()?;
        Ok(FixResult::Fixed("Rebuilt blocked issues cache".into()))
    }
}
```

#### FTS5 Index Check
```rust
impl HealthCheck for FtsCheck {
    fn run(&self, storage: &SqliteStorage) -> Result<CheckResult> {
        let sql = "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM issues WHERE id NOT IN (SELECT id FROM issues_fts)";
        let missing = storage.query_single::<i64>(sql)?;
        if missing > 0 {
            return Ok(CheckResult::Error(format!("{} issues missing from FTS", missing)));
        }
        Ok(CheckResult::Ok)
    }
    
    fn fix(&self, storage: &mut SqliteStorage) -> Result<FixResult> {
        storage.rebuild_fts_index()?;
        Ok(FixResult::Fixed("Rebuilt FTS index".into()))
    }
}
```

## Output Formats

### Human-readable
```
Running health checks...

✓ schema        Database schema is valid
✓ refs          Referential integrity OK
⚠ blocked       Blocked cache has 3 stale entries (--fix to repair)
✓ fts           FTS5 index consistent
✓ config        Configuration valid
✓ jsonl         JSONL files consistent

Summary: 5 passed, 1 warning, 0 errors
Run with --fix to repair warnings automatically
```

### JSON
```json
{
  "checks": [
    { "name": "schema", "status": "ok" },
    { "name": "blocked", "status": "warning", "message": "3 stale entries" }
  ],
  "summary": { "passed": 5, "warnings": 1, "errors": 0 },
  "fixable": true
}
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Run all checks by default
- [ ] Schema integrity check
- [ ] Referential integrity check (deps, labels point to valid issues)
- [ ] Blocked cache consistency check
- [ ] FTS5 index consistency check
- [ ] Configuration validity check
- [ ] JSONL sync status check
- [ ] --fix flag to auto-repair
- [ ] --check to run specific checks
- [ ] --skip to exclude checks
- [ ] Human and JSON output

## Unit Tests
- All checks pass on healthy database
- Schema check detects missing table
- Ref check detects orphaned dependency
- Blocked cache check detects stale entry
- FTS check detects missing issue
- Fix flag repairs issues
- Multiple checks can be selected/skipped

## Dependencies
- SQLite Storage Layer Core
- JSONL Export/Import (for JSONL check)
- Blocked Cache Rebuild

## Rationale
The doctor command is essential for troubleshooting. Users can run it when they suspect corruption or after problematic operations. Auto-fix capability reduces manual intervention.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"feature","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T07:18:34.411206677Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T07:36:08.565782830Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T07:36:08.565782830Z","close_reason":"Superseded by minimal doctor bead (beads_rust-2hr)","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"compacted_at_commit":"","original_size":0}
 {"id":"beads_rust-k1p0","title":"sync rejects implicit external DB-family JSONL paths","description":"Fresh-eyes audit of beads_rust-84lm traced br sync --flush-only path validation through src/cli/commands/sync.rs and config::implicit_external_jsonl_allowed(). OpenStorageResult treats an external JSONL as implicitly allowed when the DB itself is also external and the JSONL is a sibling external DB-family file, but sync::validate_sync_paths only receives args.allow_external_jsonl and rejects any JSONL outside .beads when the flag is absent. Later execute_flush also passes args.allow_external_jsonl into ExportConfig instead of the effective policy. Result: a workspace opened via an explicit external DB family can be readable/writable through config but br sync --flush-only still rejects its configured sibling JSONL unless the operator redundantly adds --allow-external-jsonl. Expected: sync path validation/export should use args.allow_external_jsonl OR config::implicit_external_jsonl_allowed(&startup.paths.beads_dir, &startup.paths.db_path, &startup.paths.jsonl_path).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","created_at":"2026-04-21T04:50:20.778647324Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-21T14:14:15.838377156Z","closed_at":"2026-04-21T14:14:15.837894201Z","close_reason":"Fixed sync external JSONL path validation to use effective DB-family policy and reject raw .git/symlink escapes before path resolution","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}

 {"id":"beads_rust-wyr","title":"Unit test coverage expansion (no mocks)","description":"# Unit Coverage Expansion

## Scope
- Add/expand unit tests across core modules without mocks/fakes.
- Use real SQLite (TempDir + .beads) and real JSONL files.

## Requirements
- Deterministic test data and timestamps.
- Avoid network access and external dependencies.
- Prefer table-driven tests to capture edge cases.

- All unit suites pass via cargo test.
- New tests include edge cases + error paths.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T16:16:41.959059236Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:25.448907681Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T17:47:25.448907681Z","close_reason":"Fixed failing tests: added sync.* prefix to is_startup_key function, fixed determine_action test calls with missing 4th argument.  {"id":"beads_rust-x1j","title":"Validate status filters for list/search","description":"List/search status filters should reject invalid status values instead of silently ignoring them.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","estimated_minutes":0,"created_at":"2026-01-16T19:15:10.714587819Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-16T19:15:21.166026386Z","closed_at":"2026-01-16T19:15:21.166026386Z","close_reason":"Completed","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0}
-{"id":"beads_rust-x34c","title":"[P2] JSONL export hydrates full workspace before writing","description":"Root cause: src/sync/mod.rs:1533-1669 and src/sync/mod.rs:1854-1930 load every export issue plus all dependency, label, and comment maps, then clone those relations into every Issue before the first JSONL line is written. src/sync/mod.rs:1717-1720 also allocates exported_ids and issue_hashes for the full workspace. On large workspaces (>10k issues with many deps/comments/labels), export can become allocation-bound and hold multiple copies of the workspace in memory.

Repro: populate 10k+ issues with labels/comments/dependencies, then run the existing Export (10k) path in benches/storage_perf.rs or run br sync --flush-only under a heap profiler; observe full-workspace hydration before streaming output.

Proposed fix: add a streaming or paged export path sorted by issue ID. Fetch relation rows by chunk or ordered cursors, normalize and serialize each issue immediately, and compute hashes/results incrementally. Keep only compact result metadata needed for safety checks.

Test/bench to add: extend benches/storage_perf.rs export coverage with relation-heavy 10k data and assert the existing <500ms target while tracking bounded peak allocation/RSS.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:15:25.941282190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:31.974817646Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":323,"issue_id":"beads_rust-x34c","author":"cod_1","text":"degraded-coordination: files: src/sync/mod.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:31Z"}]}

On large workspaces (>10k issues with many deps/comments/labels), export can become allocation-bound and hold multiple copies of the workspace in memory.

Repro: populate 10k+ issues with labels/comments/dependencies, then run the existing Export (10k) path in benches/storage_perf.rs or run br sync --flush-only under a heap profiler; observe full-workspace hydration before streaming output.

Proposed fix: add a streaming or paged export path sorted by issue ID. Fetch relation rows by chunk or ordered cursors, normalize and serialize each issue immediately, and compute hashes/results incrementally. Keep only compact result metadata needed for safety checks.

Test/bench to add: extend benches/storage_perf.rs export coverage with relation-heavy 10k data and assert the existing <500ms target while tracking bounded peak allocation/RSS.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"cod_1","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:15:25.941282190Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:00.766044109Z","closed_at":"2026-04-22T17:48:00.765728879Z","close_reason":"Streamed JSONL export in bounded issue batches rather than hydrating the full workspace before writing; verified JSONL import/export and e2e sync coverage, with existing unrelated clippy/fmt and lock-wait harness failures noted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"comments":[{"id":323,"issue_id":"beads_rust-x34c","author":"cod_1","text":"degraded-coordination: files: src/sync/mod.rs","created_at":"2026-04-22T17:35:31Z"}]}
 {"id":"beads_rust-x77f","title":"Fix benchmark_dataset_quick test failure (br not compatible with beads_rust dataset)","description":"The test benchmark_dataset_quick in tests/benchmark_datasets.rs fails with 'br not compatible with this dataset'.

Investigation by Opus-45-Claude:
- The test creates an isolated workspace by copying the beads_rust .beads directory
- It runs 'br list --json' to verify compatibility
- The command appears to work when run manually, but fails in the test context
- Stderr shows DEBUG logs being captured, first line: 'Validating sync path path=./.beads/issues.jsonl beads_dir=./.beads'
- The test checks br_check.success (exit code == 0) and fails

Root cause hypothesis: Unknown - needs further investigation. The test worked for benchmark_dataset_infrastructure_works which uses empty workspaces.

Note: This test file (tests/benchmark_datasets.rs) is untracked and was created by another agent (likely ChartreuseRidge for beads_rust-u8yr).","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"bug","assignee":"Opus-45-Claude","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T06:54:03.462621883Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T07:23:36.529017496Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T07:23:36.529017496Z","close_reason":"Test now passes consistently.  {"id":"beads_rust-x7z8","title":"Dataset registry + safe copy from real repos","description":"Create a dataset registry that uses real .beads directories as fixtures and copies them into isolated temp workspaces (no mocks, no mutation of sources).

Scope
- Register datasets: /data/projects/beads_viewer, /data/projects/coding_agent_session_search, /data/projects/brenner_bot, /data/projects/beads_rust.
- Copy .beads (and minimal repo scaffold) into temp workspace for each test run.
- Capture dataset metadata (issue count, jsonl/db sizes, dependency counts) for logging/benchmark baselines.

Acceptance
- Fixture copy is read-only to the source path and writes only to temp/target/test-datasets.
- Dataset metadata is emitted in test logs and summary.json.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","owner":"jeff141421@gmail.com","created_at":"2026-01-18T03:40:35.307131500Z","created_by":"Dicklesworthstone","updated_at":"2026-01-18T04:05:13.926239469Z","closed_at":"2026-01-18T04:05:13.926239469Z","close_reason":"Implemented dataset registry with safe copy mechanism","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7z8","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-ag35","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-08T22:47:30Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":184,"issue_id":"beads_rust-x7z8","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Notes: dataset copy should respect sync allowlist (ignore .git and non-.beads files).
